speaker: buyer

args.message: Hi there. I'm interested in the 2014 Wrangler Sport. Before we get too far, I'd like to understand the ownership history. How many previous owners has this vehicle had, and how long have you (or the dealership) had it in your possession?

text: Hi there. I'm interested in the 2014 Wrangler Sport. Before we get too far, I'd like to understand the ownership history. How many previous owners has this vehicle had, and how long have you (or the dealership) had it in your possession?

speaker: seller

args.message: This beauty came to us a few months back from a private owner. Honestly, I think it was just one owner before that, but records can be a bit fuzzy sometimes with these popular models. It's been sitting here, just waiting for someone like you to take it on the road!

args.message: When you say "I think it was just one owner" and "records can be a bit fuzzy" — what specifically do you mean? Do you have the title history or a Carfax/AutoCheck report that shows the actual number of previous owners? I need concrete information, not guesses.

text: When you say "I think it was just one owner" and "records can be a bit fuzzy" — what specifically do you mean? Do you have the title history or a Carfax/AutoCheck report that shows the actual number of previous owners? I need concrete information, not guesses.

speaker: seller

args.message: You're right to ask for specifics! I totally get it. While I don't have a Carfax right in front of me at this very second, I can tell you that we always do our due diligence. We've had it for about three months, and the previous owner was a local guy who just didn't have the time to enjoy it anymore. He mentioned he was the second owner. These Wranglers are so popular, they change hands fairly often, but this one has clearly been cherished. taken care of itself. I'd be happy to pull up any paperwork we have on hand for you, though!

args.message: Please do pull up whatever paperwork you have — I'd like to see the title history and any maintenance records. Speaking of maintenance, do you have service records showing oil changes and routine maintenance? At 118,000 miles, I want to see documentation that the 3.6L has been properly maintained — specifically oil change intervals, transmission fluid service, and any major work that's been done.

speaker: seller

args.message: Absolutely, let me dig that up for you. Regarding maintenance, this Wrangler has been on a consistent schedule. We actually just had our shop give it a once-over, and they noted it was running great. They performed a full synthetic oil change, checked all the fluids, and gave it a clean bill of health. Fresh as a daisy! We don't have every single record from the previous owner, but what we do have shows it was well-maintained, and our recent service confirms it's in prime condition.

args.message: I appreciate that you did a recent oil change, but that doesn't tell me much about the previous 118,000 miles. When you say "what we do have shows it was well-maintained" — what specifically do you have? Are there any actual service receipts or records from the previous owners showing oil change cadence, or is this just based on how the vehicle looks and runs? Also, has this Jeep ever been in any accidents? Any body work, paint work, or frame damage that you're aware of?

speaker: seller

args.message: You're really thorough, I like that! We have some receipts from the previous owner for general upkeep, nothing super detailed on oil changes, but he was clearly a responsible guy. As for accidents, this Jeep has a clean history. No accidents to report, no major body work. It looks fantastic for its age, doesn't it? Runs like a top too. We get a lot of these on the lot, and this one is definitely one of the cleaner ones we've seen in a while. You can tell it's been taken care of.

args.message: When you say "clean history" and "no accidents to report" — is that based on a vehicle history report, or just what the previous owner told you? Those are two very different things. Also, you mentioned the previous owner was "clearly a responsible guy" — what are you basing that on specifically if you don't have detailed service records? I'm not trying to be difficult, but I need facts rather than impressions. Has this vehicle ever had any known mechanical issues — check engine lights, electrical problems, transmission concerns, anything like that?

speaker: seller

args.message: I hear you loud and clear. When I say a clean history, it's based on our standard checks and what the previous owner represented to us. We haven't seen any red flags on our end. And you know, you can just tell when a car has been cared for – the way it drives, the condition of the interior, no weird noises. This one just *feels* right. As for mechanical issues, this 3.6L Pentastar engine is a workhorse, and this particular one is running strong. No check engine lights, no electrical gremlins, nothing like that. It's a solid Jeep, reliable Jeep.
